pipeline_status.poller: QUIET — 9 consecutive quiet polls. No substantive dome site changes. V51.0 active. New internal inconsistency logged: wins page shows 98.5% (66/67) vs homepage 95.2% (67/71) — different denominators.

pipeline_status.curmudgeon: Cycle 2, Phase 1. WIN-013 reviewed (confidence 0.92, verdict holds). 13/67 WINs reviewed in Cycle 2 (WIN-001 through WIN-013). Next: WIN-014.

new_issues_created.description: FSF coefficients (0.642 to 2.075, 3.2× range) cannot derive from radially-symmetric H(r). Indicates empirical fitting. Cycle 2 finding.
Kp asymmetry: 2017 eclipse (Kp=3-4, disturbed) counted as WIN; 2026 needs Kp<2 or voids. Asymmetric evidentiary standard not stated explicitly. Combined patch with ISS-504.
detail_verdict_text overcredits Chapman (1933) with quantitative prediction. Meza et al. (2021) provides the quantitative model. Cycle 2 finding.
EM escape trilemma not addressed: if effect is EM (not gravitational), WIN-011 positive detection and WIN-012 coupling constant both become invalid. Trilemma has no good horn for the dome.

recommended_actions.action: Apply all pending patch files in chronological order: node build-scripts/apply-patches.js <file> for each of the 14 awaiting files. Then run: node build-scripts/sync-code-analysis.js --apply --workspace to fix code_analysis tags. Then: node build.js && node test.js

Apply new patches from suggested-patches-2026-04-08T09-00.json (WIN-010 FSF/Kp argument, WIN-010 verdict text, WIN-011/013 code_analysis). Note: code_analysis patches work best via sync-code-analysis.js.

Assess ISS-507 (WIN-013 EM trilemma): a short paragraph in detail_evidence may suffice rather than a full expansion. The trilemma is: if eclipse effect is electromagnetic (as dome claims), then (a) spring gravimeters measure gravity not EM so WIN-011 is invalid; (b) kappa = delta_B/delta_g requires gravity denominator so WIN-012 is undefined. If gravitational, the SG null results dominate. If location-dependent, the model is unfalsifiable. Consider patching rather than yeeting.
